cowardice_flags.avoided: Mature view lectures on inclusion without naming who is responsible
cowardice_flags.should_say: BNM and successive governments pushed cashless metrics for GDP optics while under-investing in rural connectivity, merchant onboarding, basic literacy

cowardice_flags.avoided: Presents RM2.8B as 'e-wallet fraud up 85%' without clarifying most are victim-authorised scams
cowardice_flags.should_say: BNM data shows RM2.8B total scam losses, with 95% being authorised transactions where victims were socially engineered — failure is in public education and enforcement

false_balance: Reframe and mature cards downplay that DuitNow QR is actually 3 billion not 1.2 billion — security and inclusion ARE failing to keep up
audience_check: Real Malaysians would tune out — reads like polished BNM press release lecturing from above instead of speaking to daily reality of scam calls, spotty internet, data costs, kampung stalls.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Rural and heartland Malay-majority areas show slowest adoption; cashless shift becomes de-facto economic penalty for largest demographic group
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Discussion of ethnic adoption patterns politically radioactive

hard_truths_missing.truth: Urban and business-oriented Chinese segments adopt fastest, widening urban-rural wealth gap
Every party and BNM hype cashless adoption as success while underfunding rural connectivity and literacy programs
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Calling out shared institutional failure uncomfortable

gender_truths_avoided.truth: Elderly women, who handle household finances and have lower tech familiarity, are disproportionately vulnerable to social-engineering scams driving most losses
gender_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Forces acknowledgment digital transition hits demographics harder without simple bandaids

class_truths_avoided.truth: Real divide is class (B40 income, device affordability, data costs) more than rural alone — low-income urbanites also struggle while middle-class sail ahead
class_truths_avoided.why_uncomfortable: Exposes cashless push accelerating inequality under 'inclusion' guise

predictions.if_nothing_changes: 6 months: fraud losses past RM3.5B; 2 years: rural cash above 40% in East Malaysia; trust erodes
predictions.who_can_fix: BNM and federal govt via targeted rural infra, mandatory cash acceptance rules, scam literacy
predictions.why_they_wont: Incentives tied to headline adoption %, fintech lobbying

strongest_card.why: Correctly flags risk accelerating faster than volume even if soft-pedalling authorised-scam reality

weakest_card.why: Collapses into safest platitudes without accountability

overall_assessment: Cautious technocratic draft that flags real problems but lacks spine to call out inaccurate numbers, assign blame across parties for optics-over-infrastructure, or confront class/ethnic realities. Reads like it was written to be publishable rather than to force change.
